senses.gloss: to gather, assemble, keep together, to collect

examples.text: ويشار بحسب المجلة، بان اعداد إرهابيي داعش الأجانب ما زال كبيرا جدا على الرغم من التقديرات التي ترجح مقتل الآلاف منهم، حيث تظهر البيانات المجموعة من 33 دولة لها مواطنين يقاتلون مع عصابات داعش الإرهابية، ان ما يزيد عن 6000 منهم قد تمكنوا من العودة الى بلدانهم، فيما تصل اعداد الغائبين إلى الآلاف المضاعفة.
examples.english: It is shown by the magazine that the number of foreign ISIS terrorists has not ceased to be very large in spite of assessments that affirm the death of thousands of them as likely, whereas the data gathered from 33 states to which citizens fighting for terrorist ISIS groups belong reveals that more than 6000 of them have had the opportunity of returning to their homelands, while the number of absentees has reached several thousands.

senses.gloss: to have two or more negative attributes at the same time; to have a negative attribute on top of another negative attribute

examples.english: Not only was he ignorant but also ill-mannered

senses.gloss: plural, a form of a noun denoting multiples by its sense
lumping together, putting subjects in the same category
a proposition in which a general and a particular subject are included in one category or judgement through a common attribute

etymology.text:
Compare Harsusi [script needed] (egtōma), Mehri عَتمَأ (gatmaʾ).

Intensive of جَمَعَ (jamaʕa).
